description: A Cognitive Psychologist needs you to re-analyze conflict adaptation data from a Simon Task study to explicitly exclude post-error trials. Using the trial-level data at ~/pebl/data/simon_data.csv (columns: participant_id, block, trial, congruency, acc, rt_ms), compute the Congruency Sequence Effect (Gratton Effect) for each valid participant. Exclude participant 'sub-999' as an automated bot artifact. For all other participants, calculate mean RTs for the four sequence transitions (cC, cI, iC, iI) based on the immediately preceding trial within the same block, excluding the first trial of each block, current error trials, and post-error trials. Calculate the Gratton Effect as (rt_cI - rt_cC) - (rt_iI - rt_iC). Produce a JSON report at ~/pebl/analysis/gratton_report.json containing a 'participants' list with individual cell means and effects, the exclusion details for 'sub-999' (including 'excluded': true and a 'reason' string), and the 'group_mean_gratton' across all valid participants.
